data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at 10:15 a.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was up 0.5% to 75,870, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 rose 0.5% to 23,026, led by a rise in information technology stocks after the U.S. Federal Reserve maintained its rate-cut projections for this year.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee rose 0.2% versus the U.S. dollar to 86.2350, boosted by dollar inflows and cutting of short bets against the local unit.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN067934G=CC) was quoted at 100.985 rupees, with the yield down 2 bps at 6.6483%, tracking a similar move in U.S. Treasury yields after the Federal Reserve maintained its projection of two interest rate cuts in 2025.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was little changed at 6.085%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) was nearly flat at 5.89%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 6.45%, and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 6.28%.

data.title: China, HK stocks decline as investors turn cautious after strong gains
data.description:
China and Hong Kong stocks fell on Thursday as investors turned wary of short-term volatility after a surge in tech stocks and the Hang Seng Index hitting a three-year high.

** At the midday break, the Shanghai Composite index was down 0.07% at 3,424.16 points.

** China's blue-chip CSI300 index was down 0.39%, with its financial sector sub-index lower by 0.87%, consumer staples dropping 1.15% and healthcare off 0.39%. Real estate edged up 0.1%.

** Hong Kong's benchmark Hang Seng Index declined 1.18% to 24,477.72.

** Hong Kong-listed tech giants extended losses, retreating 1.7% by the lunch break.

** Hong Kong stocks had accumulated significant gains in the previous session, and, as a result, some investors took profits, which is quite normal, said Kenny Ng, securities strategist at China Everbright Securities International.

** The major resistance for the Hang Seng in the short term is 25,000 points, he added.

** Index heavyweight Tencent (.0700HK) lost 2.6% after the internet giant, late on Wednesday, forecast a "low teens" rise in capital expenditure this year.

** The price-to-earnings ratio of the MSCI China Index reached its previous peak of around 12 times and that valuation is unlikely to improve further if economic growth remains weak, BofA Securities analysts said in a note this week.

** The smaller Shenzhen index was up 0.21%, the start-up board ChiNext Composite index was weaker by 0.14% and Shanghai's tech-focused STAR50 was down 0.16%​.

** Additionally, China left benchmark lending rates unchanged after the U.S. Federal Reserve held interest rates.

** In Hong Kong, where the local currency is pegged to the U.S. dollar, its de facto central bank warned rates will remain at relatively high levels for some time.

** Around the region, MSCI's Asia ex-Japan stock index (.MIAPJ0000PUS) was firmer by 0.30% while Japan's Nikkei index was down 0.25%.

data.title: UK Stocks-Factors to watch on March 20
data.description:
Britain's FTSE 100 CURRENCYCOM:UK100 index is seen opening down on Thursday, with futures down 0.19%.

* INTEREST RATES: The Bank of England looks set to keep interest rates on hold as it awaits the impact of U.S. President Donald Trump's trade tariffs and the British government's imminent tax hike for employers.

* PHARMACEUTICALS: The pharmaceutical industry blasted the UK government's levy that aims to curb the national health system's drugs bill.

* PRUDENTIAL: Prudential posted a rise in 2024 adjusted operating profit before tax, as the insurer benefited from strong performance across Asia and Africa.

* HSBC: HSBC Holdings is in advanced talks to sell its German fund administration business to private-equity firm BlackFin Capital Partners, Bloomberg News reported.

* REEVES: British finance minister Rachel Reeves is expected to announce next week that she has rebuilt a 9.9 billion pound($12.87 billion) fiscal buffer, Bloomberg reported.

* BURGER KING: Burger King UK is initiating discussions with lenders for a major refinancing to fund its business plan, Sky News reported.

* ARGENTA: Argenta Private Capital has raised more than 34 million pounds in third-party capital to support Convex Syndicate 1984.

* COMMODITIES: Oil prices rose on Thursday, while gold rose to an all-time high and copper prices hit their highest in more than five months.

* EX-DIVS: Hikma Pharmaceuticals , Beazley , Pearson and Chemring Group will trade without entitlement to its latest dividend pay-out on Thursday.

* The UK blue-chip index CURRENCYCOM:UK100 closed flat on Wednesday.

Tencent Holdings Target Price Raised to HK$670.00 From HK$565.00 by Daiwa >0700.HK
New Zealand May Continue Rate Cuts Despite Stronger GDP Print — Market Talk
data.description: New Zealand's stronger-than-expected 4Q GDP data shouldn't keep the country's central bank from handing down a few more rate cuts this year, says Abhijit Surya, senior APAC economist at Capital Economics. The 3.75% official cash rate at present is still in restrictive territory, he says in a note. The economy continues to bear scars from the Reserve Bank of New Zealand's previous tightening cycle, with output in the secondary sector falling by 0.8% on quarter, its sixth consecutive quarterly decline, he says. While the impact of recent monetary loosening should continue to support recovery in the coming quarters, the risks to CE's forecast for a terminal policy rate of 2.25% remain tilted to the upside, he adds. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

data.title: Zeekr 2024 Loss/Shr CNY2.73 Vs. Loss CNY4.17 >ZK
Great Southern Mining Hits Shallow Gold Mineralization at Western Australia Project
data.description:
Great Southern Mining's reverse circulation drilling program early this year at the Duketon project in Western Australia returned shallow gold mineralization based on results from initial assays, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

Results from shallow drill holes at the Golden Boulder prospect include 5 meters at 14.6 grams per tonne grade of gold from 41 meters and 8 meters at 1.8 grams per tonne grade of gold from 27 meters.

Assays from 20 holes from Golden Boulder and other regional gold targets are pending.

data.title: Press Metal Aluminium Closes MYR1.5 Billion Islamic Debt Issue
data.description:
Press Metal Aluminium completed its fifth issuance of Islamic debt, Sukuk Wakalah under its Sukuk Program, raising an aggregate nominal value of 1.5 billion ringgit.

The sukuk has tenures of 7, 10, and 15 years, according to a Wednesday Malaysian bourse filing.

Maybank Investment Bank acted as the sole Principal Adviser and Lead Arranger for the programme, while AmInvestment Bank and Maybank Investment Bank served as joint lead managers for the issuance.

data.title: Judo Capital Raised to Add From Hold by Morgans >JDO.AU
Microsoft Malaysia to launch three data centres in Q2 2025
data.description:
Microsoft will launch its first cloud region in Malaysia with three data centres by mid-year, the tech company said on Thursday, nearly a year after it announced a $2.2 billion investment in the Southeast Asian country.

The data centres, known as the Malaysia West cloud, will be located in the greater Kuala Lumpur area and will start operations in the second quarter, Laurence Si, managing director of Microsoft Malaysia, said at a press conference.

Microsoft did not reveal the capacity of the data centres.

Asked if Microsoft has faced any issues in exporting chips to Malaysia due to U.S. export controls on semiconductor chips, Si said the company was monitoring the situation.

"At this point, everything is status quo for us. We do have a good relationship with all the various stakeholders. Everything that we intend to invest and go live with in our region, that continues to stay on track," he said.

In May last year, Microsoft announced an investment of $2.2 billion over the next four years to expand its cloud and artificial intelligence services in Malaysia.

Si said Microsoft estimated its commitments in Malaysia over the next four years would generate $10.9 billion in revenues and create more than 37,000 jobs.

"Local businesses and organisations will be able to innovate faster and more securely, driving the country's progress towards becoming the hub for cloud and AI growth in Southeast Asia", he said.

data.title: Turkey - Factors To Watch on March 20
data.description:
Here are news, reports and events that may affect Turkish financial markets on Thursday.

The lira traded at 38.0000 against the U.S. dollar at 0435 GMT. On Wednesday, the lira tumbled to a record low of 42 per dollar before recouping most of the day's losses, after authorities detained President Tayyip Erdogan's main political rival.

The main BIST 100 share index closed 8.72% lower at 9,860.29 points on Wednesday.

GLOBAL MARKETS

U.S. stocks bounced on Wednesday after the U.S. Federal Reserve left its key interest rate unchanged but hinted it would reduce borrowing costs by half a percentage point by the end of the year.

DETENTION OF ISTANBUL MAYOR

Turkey seized the construction company co-owned by detained Istanbul Mayor Ekrem Imamoglu, the Istanbul Chief Public Prosecutor's office said.

PRESIDENT ERDOGAN

President Tayyip Erdogan will attend a meeting of the National Security Council. He will attend the iftar program with previous term deputies. (1200/1607)

FOREIGN MINISTER

Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan discussed the latest situation in Gaza in a phone call with an official from Palestinian militant group Hamas, Khalil al-Hayya, a Turkish foreign ministry source said.

CORN

Turkey's government has announced a 1 million metric ton import quota for animal feed corn, the trade ministry said in the Turkish official gazette on Wednesday.

Euronext wheat futures rallied on Wednesday as news that Turkey was easing import restrictions boosted demand prospects.

UNEMPLOYMENT

Turkish Statistics Institute will announce unemployment data for 2024.

RESERVES

Turkey's central bank sold a record amount of foreign currency on Wednesday after the lira dropped more than 12% following the detention of Istanbul's mayor, according to calculations by three bankers.

UEFA NATIONS LEAGUE

Turkey will host Hungary in Istanbul for Thursday's first leg of their UEFA Nations League playoff tie.

data.title: Empower Resumes Trading on Dubai Bourse
data.description:
Emirates Central Cooling Systems (DFM:EMPOWER), d/b/a Empower, resumed trading on the Dubai Financial Market on Wednesday, according to a same-day notice.

The trading suspension started earlier in the day and was lifted after the cooling services company published the results of its general assembly.

data.title: FX option expiries for 20 March 10am New York cut
data.description:
There are just a couple to take note of on the day, as highlighted in bold.

They are both for EUR/USD at the 1.0900-10 levels. As such, the expiries could once again act as more of a magnet in keeping price action drawn closer to the figure level before rolling off later in the day.

The focus in European trading will be on the SNB and BOE policy decisions, so there might not be too much in it to shake things up for the euro. The overall risk mood is holding up after the rebound yesterday as well, so there's that to factor in as well in the session ahead.

For more information on how to use this data, you may refer to this post here.

data.title: Italy Investigates Eni Over Fuel Depot Explosion
data.description:
Prosecutors in Italy launched an investigation into Eni (ENI.MI) and seven of the company's employees concerning the deadly explosion of a fuel depot near Florence, Italy, Reuters reported Wednesday, citing the prosecutors.

The prosecutors are investigating the Italian energy company and its staff on suspicion of manslaughter and other crimes. They said the explosion in December 2024 was due to the company allowing maintenance work and fuel loading operations to be carried out simultaneously.

Eni assured full cooperation in the investigation. It also reiterated that it will compensate relatives of the five people who died, as well as others who have suffered economic harm due to the incident.

Eni did not immediately respond to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

data.title: HeiTech Padu CEO Resigns
data.description:
HeiTech Padu's chief executive officer, Hasrul Azuan Bin Mohd Yusof, has resigned to pursue other career opportunities.

The board has formally started the process of finding his replacement, according to a Wednesday Malaysian bourse filing.

data.title: U. H. Zaveri Gets Board Nod to Raise Up To INR250 Million Through Rights Issue; Shares Up 4%
data.description:
Gems and jewelry company U. H. Zaveri has received board approval to raise up to 250 million Indian rupees through rights issue of shares, according to a Wednesday filing to the BSE.

The number of equity shares and the ratio of the issue will be decided later.

Shares of the company were up 4% in recent trade.

data.title: China's Baidu denies data breach after executive's daughter leaks personal info
data.description:
Chinese search giant Baidu on Wednesday denied allegations it had suffered an internal data breach after a top executive's teenage daughter posted personal details of other internet users online, sparking a controversy.

The company, one of China's largest cloud providers, was thrust into the spotlight last week after online users accused the teenage daughter of Baidu vice president Xie Guangjun of posting personal information of other users such as their phone numbers after getting into an online argument.

Baidu said all employees and executives at all levels were prohibited from accessing user data and the information posted by the teenager originated from illegally obtained "doxing databases" on foreign platforms, which aggregate stolen private data.

Baidu also said it had filed a police report regarding false information circulating online, including claims the teenager had admitted her father had provided her with database access, as the controversy over the incident continued to swell.

Xie, who is part of Baidu's cloud division, had on Monday apologised for his daughter's behaviour, saying she had obtained the information from overseas social networking sites, according to Chinese media outlets citing a post he had made to his personal WeChat feed.

Xie did not immediately respond to requests for comment via Baidu and his LinkedIn profile.

China has in recent years introduced comprehensive data laws and tightened restrictions on the sharing of private data, amid the rise of shadowy online data sellers that buy and sell personal details such as birth dates, car and home ownership.

Shares in Baidu fell more than 4% in Hong Kong trading on Thursday morning.

data.title: Zeekr 4Q Deliveries 79,250 Units, Doubling on Year >ZK
Shaftesbury Capital in Talks for Potential Team-up with Norges Bank Investment
data.description:
Shaftesbury Capital (, SHC.JO) confirmed on Wednesday that it is in advanced discussions regarding a potential long-term partnership with Norges Bank Investment Management.

If pursued, the partnership will concern the British real estate investment trust's Covent Garden portfolio. The pension fund manager will acquire 25% of the portfolio at the 2024-end valuation of 2.7 billion pounds sterling. Shaftesbury cautioned that there is no guarantee that a deal will be finalized.

The real estate company will retain control and management of the portfolio. It said the partnership will allow it to pursue long-term value creation and growth opportunities.

data.title: Winchester, US Geoscience Firm Apply for Peru Exploration Rights Agreements
data.description:
Winchester Energy and US-based geoscience firm Jaguar Exploration jointly applied to enter into technical evaluation agreements (TEAs) for two offshore exploration blocks in Peru's Lima and Pisco Basins, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

Under the agreement, if TEAs for Block PI-XP-001 and Block Pisco Oeste are granted, Winchester and Jaguar will form joint ventures with an 80% and 20% initial stake, respectively, the filing said.

Winchester has paid Jaguar $12,500 and will pay $40,000 to cover TEA application costs; $270,000 in cash; and $300,000 in Winchester shares, subject to shareholder approval, the filing added.

If only one TEA is awarded, Winchester will pay $229,500 in cash and $255,000 in shares, the company said.

Jaguar also grants Winchester exclusivity until binding agreements are finalized, expected upon the receipt of the TEA grants.

The TEAs allow two years for geological and geophysical studies using existing data, the company expects to invest about $250,000 per block during this period, the company added.

After the evaluation period, the TEA will grant the parties the right to enter into a license contract for hydrocarbon exploration and exploitation.

data.title: Zeekr January Deliveries 11,942 Vehicles >ZK
Mair Group Posts Higher FY24 Profit, Revenue
data.description:
Mair Group (ADX:MAIR) said Thursday that profit and revenue for full-year 2024 grew year over year.

Profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 171.1 million Emirati dirhams, compared with 25.9 million dirhams earlier. EPS moved to 0.08 dirham from 0.03 dirham.

The Abu Dhabi-listed food, retail, and property investor's revenue was 2.01 billion dirhams, compared with 1.35 billion dirhams a year ago.

data.title: Torrent Power Arm Forms Two New Special Purpose Vehicles
data.description:
Torrent Power unit Torrent Green Energy has incorporated two new companies, Torrent Urja 39 and Torrent Urja 40, as special purpose vehicles, according to a Wednesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The special purpose vehicles are formed to manufacture and deal in hydrogen, its by-products and or its derivatives like ammonia, chemicals, compressed natural gas and mineral substances.

The companies could also deal in the generation and distribution of other energy sources such as compressed natural gas and mineral substances, and other forms of non-conventional, renewable, green, hydro power, among others including electrical power and energy in all aspects.

data.title: Malaysia's Trade Surplus Rises 12.2% in February
data.description:
Malaysia's trade balance continued to be a surplus of 12.6 billion ringgit in February, up 12.2% from 11.2 billion ringgit a year earlier, according to data released by the country's Department of Statistics on Thursday.

Exports rose 6.2% year over year in February to 118.3 billion ringgit. Imports also rose 5.5% year on year to 105.6 billion ringgit.

Total trade increased 5.9% to 223.9 billion ringgit in February, from 211.5 billion ringgit a year ago, the data showed.

data.description:
